contract_types.name: Master Service Agreement
contract_types.description: Umbrella agreement governing the overall commercial relationship between a service provider and customer. Establishes the general terms and conditions that apply to all services procured under the agreement. Individual projects, engagements, or service lines are defined in separate Statements of Work (SOWs) or Order Forms that incorporate the MSA terms by reference.

contract_types.name: Statement of Work / Order Form
contract_types.description: Project-specific document executed under a Master Service Agreement that defines the scope, deliverables, timelines, pricing, and acceptance criteria for a specific engagement or service line. Incorporates the general terms of the MSA by reference and may include project-specific terms that supplement or modify the MSA for the particular engagement.

contract_types.name: Service Level Agreement
contract_types.description: Defines the performance standards, measurement methodology, reporting requirements, and remedies for the services provided under an MSA or SaaS subscription. Specifies uptime commitments, response and resolution times, service credits, and exclusions. May be a standalone agreement or a schedule to the MSA.

contract_types.name: Professional Services Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement for the provision of professional, consulting, advisory, or implementation services. Covers the engagement scope, deliverables, resource commitments, and intellectual property ownership for project-based work. May be standalone or operate under an MSA framework.

contract_types.name: Consulting Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement for the provision of expert consulting, advisory, or strategic services. Similar to a professional services agreement but typically focused on advisory deliverables rather than implementation work. Covers the engagement scope, consultant qualifications, intellectual property, and confidentiality.

contract_types.name: Outsourcing Agreement
contract_types.description: Long-term agreement for the outsourcing of business processes, IT operations, or other operational functions to a third-party provider. Covers service scope, transition, service levels, governance, personnel matters (including TUPE/employee transfers), business continuity, exit management, and regulatory compliance. Typically the most complex commercial agreement type.

contract_types.name: Maintenance and Support Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement for ongoing maintenance, support, and updates for software, hardware, or infrastructure. Defines support tiers, response and resolution times, update/upgrade entitlements, and end-of-life provisions. May be standalone or a schedule to a software license agreement.

contract_types.name: Managed Services Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement for the ongoing management and operation of IT infrastructure, applications, or business processes by a third-party provider. The provider assumes operational responsibility and is measured against defined service levels. Covers service scope, SLAs, governance, security, business continuity, and exit management.

contract_types.name: SaaS Subscription Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement for access to and use of cloud-based software on a subscription basis. Covers the service scope, subscription terms, SLAs, data processing, acceptable use, intellectual property, and subscription management. The customer does not receive a copy of the software but accesses it via the internet.

contract_types.name: On-Premise Software License Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement granting the customer a license to install and use proprietary software on-premise or in the customer's own infrastructure. Covers the license scope and restrictions, delivery, acceptance testing, warranty, maintenance and support, intellectual property, and compliance with license terms.

contract_types.name: Employment Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement governing the employment relationship between an employer and employee. Covers the role, responsibilities, compensation, benefits, working conditions, restrictive covenants, intellectual property assignment, confidentiality, and termination provisions. Must comply with applicable employment legislation.

contract_types.name: Independent Contractor Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement for the engagement of an independent contractor or freelancer. Defines the scope of work, deliverables, payment terms, and the independent contractor relationship. Must be carefully structured to avoid misclassification as employment, including provisions for control, economic dependence, and integration.

contract_types.name: Secondment Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement governing the temporary assignment of an employee from one organisation (the secondor) to work under the direction and control of another organisation (the secondee). Covers the assignment terms, reporting lines, responsibilities, costs, intellectual property created during the secondment, and return provisions.

contract_types.name: Intern / Work Experience Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement governing the terms of an internship or work experience placement. Covers the placement duration, learning objectives, supervision arrangements, compensation (if applicable), intellectual property, and confidentiality. Must comply with applicable minimum wage and employment legislation.
contract_types.typical_parties: Host Organisation <-> Intern (and potentially educational institution)

contract_types.name: IP Assignment Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement transferring ownership of specified intellectual property rights from the assignor to the assignee. Covers the identification of IP being assigned, the assignment mechanics (present vs future), consideration, warranties (title, non-infringement, freedom from encumbrances), and confirmatory assignment obligations.

contract_types.name: Exclusive IP License Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement granting the licensee exclusive rights to use, exploit, and commercialise specified intellectual property within a defined scope (territory, field of use, duration). The licensor agrees not to grant similar rights to any other party and, depending on the terms, may also agree not to exploit the IP itself within the exclusive scope.

contract_types.name: Non-Exclusive IP License Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement granting the licensee non-exclusive rights to use specified intellectual property. The licensor retains the right to grant similar licenses to other parties and to exploit the IP itself. Less restrictive and typically less expensive than exclusive licenses.

contract_types.name: Open Source License Agreement
contract_types.description: License agreement governing the use, modification, and distribution of open source software. Terms are defined by the applicable open source license (e.g., MIT, Apache 2.0, GPL, LGPL, AGPL). Covers the rights granted, conditions on use and redistribution, attribution requirements, and copyleft obligations (if applicable).
contract_types.typical_parties: Open Source Project / Maintainer <-> User / Contributor
regulatory_drivers: EU Cyber Resilience Act (for commercial use)

contract_types.name: Open Source Contribution / CLA
contract_types.description: Contributor License Agreement governing contributions of code, documentation, or other intellectual property to an open source project. Specifies the rights granted by the contributor (assignment or license), patent grants, representations of originality, and the contributor's warranty that they have the right to make the contribution.

contract_types.name: Data Processing Agreement (GDPR)
contract_types.description: Agreement required by GDPR Art 28(3) governing the processing of personal data by a processor on behalf of a controller. Covers the subject matter and duration of processing, the nature and purpose of processing, the type of personal data and categories of data subjects, and the obligations and rights of the controller. Must include all mandatory provisions of Art 28(3)(a)-(h).

contract_types.name: Standard Contractual Clauses (Module 2: C2P)
contract_types.description: EU Standard Contractual Clauses for the transfer of personal data from a controller in the EEA to a processor in a third country. The most commonly used SCC module for technology services. Based on the European Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2021/914. Supplements the DPA for international data transfers.
contract_types.typical_parties: Data Exporter (Controller in EEA) <-> Data Importer (Processor in third country)

contract_types.name: Standard Contractual Clauses (Module 1: C2C)
contract_types.description: EU Standard Contractual Clauses for the transfer of personal data between two controllers, one in the EEA and one in a third country. Used for controller-to-controller data sharing arrangements, joint marketing, and inter-company transfers within corporate groups.
contract_types.typical_parties: Data Exporter (Controller in EEA) <-> Data Importer (Controller in third country)

contract_types.name: UK International Data Transfer Agreement
contract_types.description: UK-specific data transfer mechanism for transferring personal data from the UK to third countries. The UK IDTA or UK Addendum to the EU SCCs replaces the EU SCCs for UK-originating transfers following Brexit. Approved by the UK Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) and required under UK GDPR.

contract_types.name: HIPAA Business Associate Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement required by HIPAA when a covered entity engages a business associate that will create, receive, maintain, or transmit protected health information (PHI) on behalf of the covered entity. Must include specific provisions required by 45 CFR 164.504(e) covering permitted uses, safeguards, breach notification, and termination.

contract_types.name: Data Sharing Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement governing the sharing of personal data or other datasets between two or more controllers for specified purposes. Defines the data to be shared, the purposes of sharing, each party's controller obligations, data quality standards, retention periods, and individual rights management. Used for research collaborations, joint marketing, and inter-organisational data exchange.
contract_types.typical_parties: Data Controller A <-> Data Controller B

contract_types.name: Reseller Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement authorising a reseller to market, sell, and distribute the principal's products or services to end customers within a defined territory or market segment. Covers the reseller appointment (exclusive or non-exclusive), pricing and margins, ordering and fulfilment, branding, end-customer terms, and termination.

contract_types.name: Distribution Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement appointing a distributor to purchase and resell the supplier's products within a defined territory. The distributor typically buys products at wholesale prices and resells at its own prices. Covers territory rights (exclusive or non-exclusive), minimum purchase commitments, pricing, logistics, marketing obligations, and termination.

contract_types.name: OEM Agreement
contract_types.description: Original Equipment Manufacturer agreement authorising the OEM partner to embed, bundle, or integrate the licensor's technology, components, or software into the OEM's own products for sale to end customers. Covers the license grant, integration requirements, quality standards, royalties, branding, and end-customer terms.

contract_types.name: Joint Venture Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement between two or more parties to establish and operate a joint business venture, sharing resources, risks, profits, and governance. Covers the formation and structure of the JV entity, capital contributions, management and governance, profit/loss sharing, intellectual property contributions and ownership, transfer restrictions, and exit mechanisms.
contract_types.typical_parties: JV Partner A <-> JV Partner B (and potentially JV Entity)

contract_types.name: Strategic Partnership Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement establishing a strategic business partnership between two or more organisations for mutual benefit. Less formal than a joint venture (typically no separate entity created). Covers the partnership objectives, roles and responsibilities, resource commitments, go-to-market strategy, revenue sharing, intellectual property, and governance.

contract_types.name: Consortium Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement between multiple parties forming a consortium to jointly bid for and deliver a project or contract. Covers the consortium structure, lead member responsibilities, work allocation, pricing, joint and several liability, internal governance, intellectual property ownership, and dissolution provisions.
contract_types.typical_parties: Lead Member <-> Consortium Member A <-> Consortium Member B

contract_types.name: Non-Disclosure Agreement (Mutual)
contract_types.description: Bilateral confidentiality agreement where both parties disclose and receive confidential information. Used at the outset of commercial discussions, due diligence processes, or partnership explorations where both parties will share sensitive business information. Defines what constitutes confidential information, permitted uses, standard exceptions, and the duration of obligations.
contract_types.typical_parties: Disclosing Party A <-> Disclosing Party B

contract_types.name: Non-Disclosure Agreement (One-Way)
contract_types.description: Unilateral confidentiality agreement where only one party (the disclosing party) shares confidential information and only the receiving party has confidentiality obligations. Used for vendor evaluations, RFP processes, and situations where information flow is primarily one-directional.

contract_types.name: Share Purchase Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement for the sale and purchase of shares in a company. Covers the shares being sold, purchase price and payment terms, conditions precedent, representations and warranties, indemnities, restrictive covenants, and completion mechanics. The most common structure for M&A transactions in private companies.

contract_types.name: Asset Purchase Agreement
contract_types.description: Agreement for the sale and purchase of specified business assets (rather than shares). The buyer selects and acquires specific assets and assumes specified liabilities. Covers the asset identification, purchase price allocation, conditions precedent, representations and warranties, and completion mechanics.
